prompt:
Ticket: draft pages for sitepress. Writers keep parking half-finished handbook pages on side branches because anything merged to main goes live on the next deploy. We want drafts to live on main like everything else. The codebase is the plugin-based generator in this repo (see README.md for the layout); the new acceptance tests sit in test_sitepress.ts below the 'draft pages feature' divider and pin everything end to end on the returned file map. The existing tests above the divider must stay green. Agreed behavior: 1. Config: new optional boolean `includeDrafts`, default false, validated as strictly as the existing flags — a non-boolean value is rejected with a clear error, and the key must stop tripping the unknown-key check. 2. Marking: a page whose front matter says `draft: true` is a draft; `draft: false` or no draft key means a normal page. 3. Default build (includeDrafts off): drafts vanish entirely — no output file, no nav entry, no sitemap or feed presence. Every other page renders exactly as today. 4. Preview build (includeDrafts on): drafts build at their normal paths, show up in the nav like any page, and carry a visible marker — `

Draft — not published

` as the first thing inside
. Non-draft pages must never get the banner. 5. The hard rule: sitemap.xml and feed.xml never list a draft. Those two files must come out byte-identical whether or not the build includes drafts. node --test test_sitepress.ts is the bar; the test file is read-only.
